title: At the market
setting: At the Tuesday market on the Reuss, you approach a fruit stand.

steps.speaker: Buurin
steps.es: Grüezi! Schöni Öpfel hüt, wänd Sie probiere?
steps.en: Hello! Nice apples today, would you like to try one?

options.feedback: Warm and natural — accepting a taste is exactly the right market etiquette.
Fine, though accepting the friendly offer first builds rapport. You can ask the price after.
A bit curt for a friendly farmer. A 'nei danke' would be softer.

steps.speaker: Buurin
steps.es: Drü Stutz s'Kilo. Alls vo da, regional.
steps.en: Three francs a kilo. All from here, regional.

options.es: Denn nimm ich es Kilo, bitte.
options.en: Then I'll take a kilo, please.
options.feedback: Clear and decisive — perfect market exchange.
A natural question — farmers love to talk about their produce.
Swiss markets don't haggle, and three francs is fair. If it's too much, just say 'nei danke.'

steps.speaker: Buurin
steps.es: Gärn! Susch no öppis? Ich han au schöni Bire.
steps.en: Gladly! Anything else? I also have nice pears.

options.es: Nei merci, das isch alls für hüt.
options.en: No thanks, that's all for today.
options.feedback: Polite and complete. 'Das isch alls' neatly closes the order.

options.es: Ja, au es paar Bire, bitte.
options.en: Yes, a few pears too, please.

options.feedback: Bit awkward to announce — a simple 'nei merci' is smoother.

steps.speaker: Buurin
steps.es: Da bitte, drü Stutz. Schöne Tag und merci!
steps.en: Here you go, three francs. Nice day and thanks!

options.feedback: Perfect — warm and local. The market is where you become a regular.
Too flat for such a friendly exchange. Send the warmth back.
